annotations.hia:
The person took the carrot (00:00 - 00:02).
The person took the peeler (00:04 - 00:05).
The person peeled the carrot (00:05 - 00:30).
The person put down the peeler (00:30 - 00:31).
The person took the knife (00:31 - 00:32).
The person cut the carrot (00:33 - 00:44).
annotations.caption:
[00:00 - 00:03] The user picks up a whole carrot and slides a white ceramic bowl closer. A soft thud occurs as the bowl touches the countertop, accompanied by the rustle of the carrot being handled.

[00:04 - 00:23] The person begins peeling the carrot. A series of rhythmic, crisp scraping sounds are heard as the peeler moves down the length of the carrot, with carrot skins falling into the bowl.

[00:24 - 00:40] The scraping becomes faster and more frequent, creating a continuous, high-pitched scratching texture as the user peels the remaining sides and the tip of the carrot.

[00:41 - 00:47] A distinct metallic clatter is heard as the user places the peeler on the metal countertop. This is followed by the sound of the bowl being pushed aside, creating a sliding friction noise.

[00:48 - 01:11] Using a large kitchen knife, the person begins chopping the carrot. Sharp, resonant thuds of the blade striking the plastic cutting board are heard as the carrot is cut into manageable lengths.

[01:11 - 01:16] A metallic scraping sound occurs as the user uses the side of the knife blade to gather the carrot pieces in the center of the board.

[01:16 - 01:43] The chopping accelerates into a rapid-fire sequence of percussive knocks. The sound is steady and rhythmic as the user finely juliennes the carrot slices into thin strips.

[01:44 - 01:51] Faint scraping and shuffling sounds are heard as the user adjusts the pile of carrot strips and repositions the knife for the next section.

[01:52 - 02:14] A series of crisp, rhythmic chops resumes. The intensity of the thud against the board varies slightly depending on the force used to cut through the denser parts of the carrot.

[02:14 - 02:29] The user turns the knife sideways, creating a prolonged metallic scraping sound against the textured surface of the white cutting board while sliding carrot strips to one side.

[02:30 - 02:49] Rapid, brisk cutting sounds continue as the last segment of the carrot is sliced. The tempo is high, producing a quick, repetitive clicking pattern.

[02:50 - 03:03] The task concludes with a few final, soft scrapes of the blade against the board to tidy up the pile of julienned carrots.

qa_pairs.context: [00:00] The user picks up a whole carrot and slides a white ceramic bowl closer. [00:02] A soft thud occurs as the bowl touches the countertop, accompanied by the rustle of the carrot being handled.
qa_pairs.question: How many distinct environmental sound events occur between 00:00 and 00:03 as the user prepares the items?
qa_pairs.answer: Two distinct sounds occur: a soft thud from the ceramic bowl touching the countertop and the rustle of the carrot being handled.

qa_pairs.context: [00:24] The scraping becomes faster and more frequent, creating a continuous, high-pitched scratching texture as the user peels the remaining sides.
qa_pairs.question: What are the acoustic qualities and texture of the peeling sound as the user's speed increases between 00:24 and 00:40?
qa_pairs.answer: The sound is described as having a continuous, high-pitched scratching texture.

qa_pairs.context: [00:41] A distinct metallic clatter is heard as the user places the peeler on the metal countertop.
qa_pairs.question: What was the source and relative location of the metallic clatter heard at 00:41?
qa_pairs.answer: The sound was caused by the peeler being placed down onto the metal countertop.

qa_pairs.context: [00:48] Using a large kitchen knife, the person begins chopping the carrot. [00:50] Sharp, resonant thuds of the blade striking the plastic cutting board are heard.
qa_pairs.question: What objects are interacting to produce the 'sharp, resonant thuds' heard starting at 00:48?
qa_pairs.answer: The sounds are produced by a large kitchen knife blade striking a plastic cutting board.

qa_pairs.context: [01:11] A metallic scraping sound occurs as the user uses the side of the knife blade to gather the carrot pieces in the center of the board.
qa_pairs.question: Why does a metallic scraping sound occur between 01:11 and 01:16, based on the user's actions?
qa_pairs.answer: The sound occurs because the user is using the side of the knife blade to gather the already chopped carrot pieces into the center of the cutting board.

qa_pairs.context: [01:52] A series of crisp, rhythmic chops resumes. [02:00] The intensity of the thud against the board varies slightly depending on the force used to cut through the denser parts of the carrot.
qa_pairs.question: How does the physical property of the carrot being cut influence the audio characteristics of the thuds heard between 01:52 and 02:14?
qa_pairs.answer: The intensity of the audio thuds varies based on the force required to cut through different sections, specifically becoming more intense when cutting through the denser parts of the carrot.

qa_pairs.context: [02:14] The user turns the knife sideways, creating a prolonged metallic scraping sound against the textured surface of the white cutting board.
qa_pairs.question: What is the duration and nature of the sound produced when the user moves the carrot strips at 02:14?
qa_pairs.answer: It is a prolonged metallic scraping sound that lasts until approximately 02:29.

qa_pairs.context: [02:30] Rapid, brisk cutting sounds continue as the last segment of the carrot is sliced. [02:35] The tempo is high, producing a quick, repetitive clicking pattern.
qa_pairs.question: Based on the high-tempo clicking pattern heard starting at 02:30, what visual action is the user performing?
qa_pairs.answer: The user is performing rapid, brisk cutting of the final segment of the carrot.
